productDecision.decision: FLOW-21 Dynamic Forms & Workflows can proceed through the mobility/auth protocol after repairing the pre-existing service inheritance and tenant-context coupling defects.
notes: Functional spec marks FLOW-21 half-built from a product/UI standpoint: server-side schema, submission, automation, and analytics services exist, while the public form-filler route/page is still a product implementation gap.

Role analysis identifies 2 full cells and 5 partial cells. Mobility/auth certification can proceed on the service bundle while UI evidence must cover public, tenant-user, tenant-admin, freelancer, business-partner, event-organiser, and platform-admin surfaces.

changes: Extended all four FLOW-21 services from MicroserviceBase with T629-T632 service descriptors.
Replaced direct ClsService and TENANT_CONTEXT_KEY imports with TenantContextResolver.
Kept a legacy get(tenant) fallback so existing test harnesses remain compatible while production paths use the resolver.
Renamed injected database and queue members to dbFabric and queueFabric to avoid MicroserviceBase accessor collisions.
Updated source-inspection tests so they assert the resolver-based tenant context contract and MicroserviceBase inheritance.
